title: Solid technology but...
pros: -The true culture of the company is that most employees will help one another without any pushback. -The technology is solid. ServiceNow has a pretty full toolbox to help IT shops with their needs. -Nice offices
cons: -The sales teams are weak. They rely on the ITSM install base and hardly have relationships outside of IT. -The company is frugal beyond belief. While the executives are touting the cash reserves to Wall St., they leave out that that is at the expense of the employees. -Leadership is rife with favoritism. Star performers get "solid" ratings and minimal raises, while buddies take care of their buddies whether they perform well or not. -While ServiceNow publicly touts no layoffs, if an employee is rated with a score of 1 or 2 (out of 5), they can be fired without any notice. These can easily be looked at as quiet layoffs without the need to pay severance. (While this did not happen to me, but I saw it happen multiple times)

title: Trapped in a Walled Garden: The Hidden Career Risk of Platform-Specific Development
body: Trapped in a Walled Garden: The Hidden Career Risk of Platform-Specific Development
pros: Great place to work on. They follow a process, Policies. Respectful mentality. Many Benefits.
cons: Prior to the acquisition, I was a versatile developer working across a broad technology stack with a large, active community. However, ServiceNow's closed ecosystem enforces a platform-specific development model, leaving little room to grow beyond its proprietary boundaries. This significantly narrows my marketability in the job market â especially now that the branch has decided to close â given that ServiceNow expertise has limited recognition and demand in my local market.

title: hidden Toxic beyond relief

Overworks you with impunity, no extra pay, no respect for off or sick days, despite all good will and weekend shifts to cover their product emergencies, salvaging their business. HR is unreachable and doesn't care, busy polishing nails. Ethics hotline is a joke, there on paper only. Even Lyra legal benefits excludes "workplace related issues" which you only find out about when actually needed. 401k matching also scams you into their funds, tethered to their poor performance, unable to contribute more to your own Fidelity BrokerageLink if your own portfolio performs better. Absolutely no avenue nor department to raise such concerns with. Inability to actually use "unlimited" vacation used as legal workaround to avoid pay.

title: Strong leadership, clear strategy, and a company that knows how to execute
body: Strong leadership, clear strategy, and a company that knows how to execute
pros: ServiceNow has some of the strongest leadership Iâve experienced, starting at the top. Bill McDermott is genuinely inspirational â he has the rare ability to energize teams while also being deeply clear on where the company is going and why. That clarity shows up everywhere. One of the biggest strengths is how effectively corporate strategy and narrative are pushed down to employees. The company does an exceptional job ensuring people at all levels understand the strategy, can articulate it, and see how their work connects to it â better than any company Iâve worked for. The organization is also able to pivot quickly when priorities shift, which keeps the work dynamic and relevant. Thereâs a strong sense of momentum, and people genuinely want to be here and contribute.
cons: Like many fast-growing companies, the pace can be intense, and priorities can shift quickly, which may feel challenging for those who prefer longer planning cycles. Alignment is strong, but execution can sometimes feel compressed as teams move fast to keep up with strategic changes.

title: Smoke & performative mirrors
pros: Typical benefits which come from most tech jobs (PTO, etc). A lot of people really trying to do good work.
cons: In some orgs UX is treated like graphic design. Senior leaders outsourcing all thinking to latest "hot" AI tool despite being hired for experience. Some groups work in a waterfall method where engineering delivers inline, non-reusable, styled code and require everything to be visualized with no ability to work in a more modern, collaborative fashion. Some design leadership have openly mocked their should-be counterparts in workshops and other visible forums. Executives give feedback or ideas without having clear goals or strategies in mind, punishing teams who don't deliver despite absence of an actual plan forward. Certain program operations teams mandate multiple meetings just to stitch information together just enough to be performative without real value. Tendencies for teams to mark initiatives as "green" to avoid being realistic about issues plaguing meaningful progress.

title: ServiceNow: An Incredible Place to Grow and Make an Impact
body: ServiceNow: An Incredible Place to Grow and Make an Impact
pros: Mission & Impact: Truly feel like I'm part of something bigger than just a job. The company's vision for making the world of work better is compelling, and you see the real-world impact every day. Growth & Momentum: ServiceNow is an absolute rocket ship. The company is constantly growing and evolving, which translates into huge opportunities for career development and learning. It feels exciting to be at a place with so much upward trajectory. Platform Excellence: The ServiceNow platform itself is outstanding. Itâs innovative, powerful, and a great product to stand behind. It makes the day-to-day work engaging. Culture & People: The culture and people are amazing. It's a supportive, collaborative, and genuinely kind environment. I've been able to connect with brilliant colleagues who are all rowing in the same direction.
cons: Pace of Change (Common in Big Tech): The only real "con," and it's common in a hyper-growth tech company, is the sheer amount of change. Whether it's organizational shifts, new product launches, or process updates, the pace can sometimes feel relentless. It requires constant adaptation, which can be challenging at times.

title: Good benefits and opportunities, but management quality varies
body: Good benefits and opportunities, but management quality varies
pros: A supportive team makes a huge difference and if you get a good team you are truly lucky. The company offers strong benefits including RSUs, learning opportunities and a solid work environment. Work culture is generally good and there is exposure to modern platforms and technologies.
cons: Some managers in the support team struggle with people management. Decision making and communication can feel inconsistent at times. Career growth may depend heavily on which manager you report to, which is not always ideal.

title: The best company I've ever worked for
body: The best company I've ever worked for
pros: Working at ServiceNow is genuinely fulfilling. I get to collaborate across time zones with passionate, talented colleagues who care deeply about our people and our purpose. One of the things I love most is how ServiceNow lives its values. Iâm proud to be involved in our Employee Belonging Groups (EBGs) and to serve as a Culture Champion, helping foster inclusion, connection, and authenticity across the business. These programs arenât just buzzwords - theyâre embedded in how we work and show up for each other. Leadership is transparent and empowering, and I feel trusted to lead meaningful projects that make a difference. Working at this rocketship, thereâs always plenty of projects to throw yourself into! ServiceNow invests in its people, celebrates wins, and encourages growth. Itâs a place where you can do your best work, be yourself, and feel part of something bigger. Love the extra Wellbeing Days - they are very much appreciated!
cons: There arenât many downsides to working at ServiceNow, but like any fast-paced company, there are a few things to be mindful of: Learning & Development: There are fantastic opportunities to grow and learn - whether through formal programs or stretch assignments. The challenge is carving out time to take full advantage of them in a busy environment. Career Progression: The promotion process can feel lengthy and complex at times. That said, there are plenty of leadership opportunities and ways to expand your impact beyond formal titles. Pace & Intensity: Itâs a dynamic, fast-moving workplace. The pace can be intense, which may not suit everyoneâbut if you thrive in a high-energy environment, youâll find it incredibly rewarding.

Company has hired a lot in past few years and has been transitioning to pay for performance. Some more tenured employees have found this adjustment hard as there is more focus on tangible results and positive behaviors. If you are comfortable with a fairly standard performance management structure - you will be fine.

title: Great People, Outdated Tech
pros: The company is a stable and prominent global player with a highly popular product, which provides a sense of security. There haven't been any layoffs in the engineering organizations for a long time, which is a huge plus. The technical challenges are genuinely interesting, and I've had the opportunity to work on some complex problems. The best part, however, is the people. My coworkers are some of the smartest and most hardworking engineers I've ever collaborated with.
cons: The tech stack is outdated and heavily proprietary, which means the skills you gain aren't easily transferable to other jobs. The code and architecture are extremely complex and convoluted, making it a painful and slow process to fix bugs and build new features. This leads to a lot of defects and impacts development speed. We operate on a long waterfall release cycle, and the company as a whole lacks agility due to its size. Teams often work in silos. Upward mobility can be a real challenge; it seems to be more about politics and appearances than technical merit.

I can say I am walking away with actual knowledge and experience gained rather than being simply drained from all the political nonsense that otherwise goes on in every corporate environment that has reached it's peak and is heading in decline.
cons: Value that was provided was not recognized by Leadership in the form of promotions, raises but work/contributions were absorbed by Leadership so that Leadership could be promoted. Much lip service paid to equal opportunities and diversity but no real action to support this. Middle Management heavy with skills focused on 'maintaining their jobs' amidst routine (quarterly) re-orgs wherein 'redundant roles' were eliminated. Much lip service is paid towards 'career goals' and supporting 'growth' but absent in evidence.

title: The world works with Servicenow and I work at, with and for Servicenow!
body: The world works with Servicenow and I work at, with and for Servicenow!
pros: Joining ServiceNow means stepping into a company that truly practices what it preaches. From day one, I felt like I had jumped onto a high-speed train â driven by innovation, precision, and purpose. The onboarding experience was world-class: structured, empowering, and deeply aligned with our mission to make work work better for everyone. Internally, we use the same cutting-edge tools and AI-powered workflows that we deliver to our customers. Everything is seamlessly organized and automated â a living example of the value our platform provides. ServiceNow is not just a tech company; itâs a global, well-orchestrated sales and marketing engine with solutions that genuinely make a difference. If you thrive in a dynamic, international, and complex software environment, this is the place to be. Youâll collaborate with some of the brightest, kindest, and most driven professionals in the industry â all united by a shared vision and a strong sense of purpose.
cons: In a company that celebrates process excellence, itâs no surprise that workflows are meticulously designed. But sometimes, the love for structure can go a step too far â and you find yourself opening a ticket for things that might just need a quick chat. A touch more pragmatism could help us stay agile without losing our operational edge. Also, the pace is exhilarating â but not without pressure. The growth ambition is bold, and the quarterly rhythm keeps us sharp. Still, in our race to scale, we should remember: even high-speed trains need smooth curves. Taking a moment to breathe, reflect, and recalibrate can help us reach our destination not just faster, but better.

pros: - The company really encourages fresh ideas, thereâs space to be creative and try new things without fear of failure. - Diversity and inclusion arenât just buzzwords here. People from all backgrounds are welcomed, respected, and given a voice. - Leadership actually listens. You feel like your input matters, and thatâs rare. - The work feels meaningful. Youâre not just clocking in, youâre contributing to something bigger. - The teams across the company are full of smart, kind, and passionate people who genuinely care about what they do. - Thereâs genuine support for growth, whether itâs through mentorship, hands-on training, or simply being trusted to explore new ideas and take on fresh challenges. - Communication is clear and honest, which makes it easier to trust the direction the company is heading. - Flexibility is a big plus. title: A once great team being dismantled
body: A once great team being dismantled
pros: Technology and strategy is still best in class. Global leadership is inspiring under CEO
cons: The team and culture in APAC which was once great is being slowly dismantled, brick by brick. A culture of fear based management is being instilled under the new APAC leadership and becoming toxic. Activity tracking and reporting is nearing on levels that would be expected by an ex-con on probation, not of a seasoned and mature sales team and this is only one component on the time wasted on admin! Blanket performance ratings are placing waves of reps onto performance management plans, without taking in to account previous attainment or expected attainment for the year. Tenured and seasoned reps are being managed despite only having one account and one transaction per year without any regard. Itâs the customers who will suffer the most, just so the APAC team can hire more robots from Oracle and destroy the team even more.

title: Are they willing to take action on EVS scores?
body: Are they willing to take action on EVS scores?
pros: Itâs clear who is fully engaged, who is curious, who is willing to put in the work, and who wants to get better. The opposite of the previous statement is also abundantly clear. Focus attention on those who are committed and disregard distractions. Solid benefits. Golden handcuffs if that's what you want for a few years.
cons: ServiceNow does not have a track record of promoting successful individual contributors into leadership roles. Leadership hires are typically external candidates with prior leadership experience or internal leaders from other product lines or verticals. Many first-line, almost all of second-line, and every third-line and above sales leader lacks sufficient understanding of the products and how the solutions integrate. Due to the lack of leaders with a semblance of technical aptitude, the development of ICs is being crippled and deals are falling apart. Too much reliance on strong product offerings vs true selling capabilities. Additionally, sales leaders struggle in customer meetings to answer simple questions about our products, which impacts credibility. A significant number might be better suited elsewhere.

title: Incredible leadership, culture, and balance
pros: ServiceNow is one of the best organizations I've worked for, even when compared to other big tech companies. Leadership is transparent, inspiring, and genuinely invested in employee wellbeing. The company takes a proactive approach to preventing burnout through generous holidays and regular company-wide Wellness Days. Flexible scheduling, remote/hybrid work options, and unlimited time off also help support a healthy work-life balance. Compensation and benefits are competitive, and there are many opportunities for professional development and internal mobility. Most importantly, ServiceNowâs inclusive culture encourages me to bring my authentic self to work every day.
cons: ServiceNow embraces bold decision-making, and leadership and organizational changes occur regularly. Most result in positive outcomes, but the rapid pace of change can present challenges.

cons: One of the quirks of this organization is the whole "hire at any level, for any role" situation. You never really know if you're hiring a Senior Staff (IC5) or a Senior Designer (IC3) â the lines can get blurry real quick. It's like the HR team is playing a game of âWhack-a-Moleâ with job titles. Depending on the situation, you might end up with someone overqualified, or just a little... underwhelming. title: Beware the OTE
pros: - I've had a long and often rewarding career at ServiceNow. - ServiceNow stuck with me through a personal tragedy which required FMLA time and inconsistent business hours for over a year. - This company is "going somewhere," and that always makes the work more interesting along the way. - I've met hundreds if not thousands of smart, interesting, caring people that really want to do their absolute best alongside one another. There are few people that are dead weight, and those types don't last long, here.
cons: - I'm getting off-shored. They can hire 3-4 people in India for what they are paying me. It isn't directly called this, but that's what is happening. - Even though I'm told my performance for 2024 was "just fine," they have held my bonus payout for 2024 as part of my transition/pending separation. It seems like a "claw back" that costs me $10-15k (after taxes). Moral of the story: They might pay you $0 for OTE (even if the company does well) if they can think of an excuse before the check is written 2 months later after what you did to earn it has happened.

title: Unprofessional Interview process - way below SaaS and Tech industry standards
body: Unprofessional Interview process - way below SaaS and Tech industry standards
pros: I applied for a role that had only been open for a few hours and reached out professionally and politely on LinkedIn to people at the office where I applied. However, I received little to no responseâeven from the recruiter responsible for the role.
cons: Entitled recruiters and future colleagues who donât take the time to respond to someone actively looking for a job, combined with a potentially biased and inexplicable recruiting process. After investing time in: Tailoring my CV Crafting an optional cover letter Reaching out to the recruiter on LinkedIn I received an automated rejection email with no explanation whatsoever, despite my profile perfectly matching all the required skills. This has been one of the worstâif not the worstâand most biased recruiting experiences Iâve had in my 10+ years working in tech. For context, I have worked for companies like META, HubSpot, and Googleâso I know what a hiring pipeline with top-tier candidates looks like. Iâm still baffled as to how ServiceNow can afford to overlook CVs that match the required skills and were submitted in a timely manner.

title: Innovative Work, but Strategy Alignment Needs Focus
body: Innovative Work, but Strategy Alignment Needs Focus
pros: ServiceNow offers exciting, high-impact work, especially in AI and digital transformation. The company excels in execution, but cross-functional strategy alignment can be unclear, sometimes leading to inefficiencies. Leadership is forward-thinking, but long-term vision and day-to-day priorities donât always connect seamlessly. A great place for those who enjoy solving complex problems in a fast-paced environment.
cons: Lack of Strategic Alignment: Teams often work in silos, making it hard to drive cohesive initiatives and long-term planning. Cross-Functional Challenges: Collaboration can be difficult due to shifting priorities and unclear ownership. Fast-Paced with High Expectations: The workload is intense, leaving little time for strategic reflection.

title: ServiceNow Costa Rica review
pros: The company is young in age and in an expansion phase of its life. Costa Rica operations are growing and expected to grow in the upcoming years. The onboarding process is ahead of other companies: Before your first day in the office, you will receive homework. There are plenty of knowledge base articles, so you can easily self-learn about the company. Internal tools are intuitive and easy to use, and ServiceNow itself is proud to be customer zero, so anything related to new tools that will be launched to the market is used first at ServiceNow. Personal time off is generous; up to 24 days are distributed in different time-off streams.
cons: The compensation package for the new employee did not include any stock-based rewards, which is different from some other companies located in Costa Rica that offer a stock hiring grant. As the company grows, there are some benefits that are still being assessed for deployment, so if you are impatient, that will cause you a headache. The company has some technology tools that are redundant in function and nature, e.g. Teams, Zoom & Slack, so it will be easier to just have 1.

title: "best place to work"?? Nothing further from truth.
body: "best place to work"?? Nothing further from truth.
pros: Other teams/groups somewhere appear to have really have actual, healthly work-life balance. Would love to learn of them for openings and transfer over ASAP.
cons: All double talk. Public non-specific praises diametrically opposite of actual labor abuses and unpaid overwork culture, coupled with lack of raises for 3rd year. What goes on internally is toxic and demoralizing, and should not be tolerated in any developed democracy. Kickoffs, quarterlies, all-hands, etc. amount to all pep rallies full of self-praise at corp level, fantastic financial stats both past and forecast, which on the front lines carrying the actual heavy load, find completely ironic, enduring perpetual excuses of lack of budget for staff, no bonuses/raises/stock grants after initial contract, coupled with unreasonably and disproportionately many duties without adequate time nor resources to perform them.
